assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: The response clearly identifies 'No channel' as legacy and explains it lacks granular exclusion types, specifically mentioning it only has the 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion available

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states in the feature comparison table that 'No minor or node upgrades' exclusion is not available on No channel (marked with ❌), and mentions per-nodepool exclusions are limited to 'no upgrades' 30 days only

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: The response clearly states in the feature comparison table that Extended support (24 months) is available on release channels (✅) but not on No channel (❌), and mentions this multiple times throughout

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: The response includes rollout sequencing in the feature comparison table showing it's available on release channels (✅) but not on No channel (❌), and mentions it as an advanced feature

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: The response recommends both Regular and Stable channels as migration targets, with specific recommendations for dev/staging (Regular) and production (Stable) clusters

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: The response includes Extended channel as 'Option A: Extended Channel (Maximum Flexibility)' and explains it provides 24-month support window for organizations with slow upgrade cycles or compliance requirements

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: The response explicitly warns that 'Some exclusion types don't translate between No channel and release channels' and states 'Only no_upgrades exclusions translate 1:1 between configurations'. It recommends adding temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion BEFORE channel migration

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: The response provides specific gcloud commands to migrate to release channels: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel regular' and similar commands for stable channel
The response does not reference or mention the specific URL cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels anywhere in the text
